fields.description:
## Why

The code-review `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar (`code_review_result_v1`) persists only the SURFACED findings
— the `blocking`, `advisory`, and `coaching` buckets — and omits the dropped / indeterminate /
overflow findings that pass-3 produced. It also does not persist the pass-1 `evidence`/`scenarios`
that `CodeFinding` carries, nor the numeric `block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled` each finding was
judged against. So "the complete pass-1 finding list" is not recoverable for a code review, unlike
plan review.

## Dependency

This story `depends_on` story A (`halfbare-tsaristic-barnacle`, 4e19). Story A adds the resolved
`block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled` to the pass-3 decision output in the **shared** review kernel
(`src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py`); because that kernel is shared, code-review findings gain
the same per-finding fields. This story CONSUMES those fields — it does not add kernel stamping. Per
the plan-review gate's dependency rule, symbols added by a `depends_on` ticket are treated as
existing.

## What

Extend the code-review sidecar to `code_review_result_v2`, pooling ALL buckets (surfaced + dropped +
indeterminate + overflow) with, per finding: the full pass-2 verification, the pass-3 determination,
the pass-1 `evidence` + `scenarios` (verified present on `CodeFinding`), and — read straight off the
decided finding — the `block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled` that dependency A stamps there.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` — bump `SCHEMA` to `code_review_result_v2`; in
  `build_payload`, pool every bucket the verdict carries (not just blocking/advisory/coaching). The
  existing `_with_norm_ids` spreads each finding dict verbatim (`{**f, "norm_id": …}`), so no field
  is stripped — persisting evidence/scenarios/block_threshold requires only that they are present on
  the finding (evidence/scenarios come from `CodeFinding`; block_threshold/blocking_enabled from
  dependency A). Keep the existing change metadata (`change_id`/`revision`/`change_fingerprint`) and
  `deps`; `latest_code_review_result` accepts both `code_review_result_v1` and `_v2`.

## Approach rationale

Mirror story A's plan-review enrichment on the code-review sidecar so both share one fidelity standard
and one reader shape, rather than inventing a code-specific record — the code-review gate runs the
identical shared four-pass kernel. Reading fields off the finding dict (rather than re-deriving them)
keeps the sidecar a pure serializer with no second source of truth.

## Compatibility & rollback

`v1` code-review sidecars remain readable (new fields/buckets absent). The novelty-floor reader that
consumes surfaced findings keeps returning the surfaced union, so region-gating is unaffected. No
backfill; reverting restores the `v1` payload. Adding fields does not change the event type or the
reducer-ignored status.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A code review that produces dropped/indeterminate findings emits a `code_review_result_v2`
      event whose payload includes those buckets, each finding with its verification, pass-3
      determination, `evidence`, `scenarios`, and (from dependency A) `block_threshold`/
      `blocking_enabled`. Oracle: an integration test driving the sidecar `emit` with a verdict whose
      findings carry those fields and asserting they all appear in the emitted event JSON.
- [ ] `latest_code_review_result` reads both a `v1` and a `v2` code-review sidecar. Oracle: a unit
      test with one hand-written record of each schema.
- [ ] The novelty-floor surfaced-findings reader returns exactly the blocking+advisory union from a
      `v2` payload, unchanged vs an equivalent `v1` payload. Oracle: a unit test comparing the two.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
